speaker: CASSIUS
text: O, look, Titinius, look, the villains fly!

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Myself have to mine own turned enemy.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: This ensign here of mine was turning back;

speaker: CASSIUS
text: I slew the coward and did take it from him.

speaker: TITINIUS
text: O Cassius, Brutus gave the word too early,

speaker: TITINIUS
text: Who, having some advantage on Octavius,

speaker: TITINIUS
text: Took it too eagerly. His soldiers fell to spoil,

speaker: TITINIUS
text: Whilst we by Antony are all enclosed.

speaker: PINDARUS
text: Fly further off, my lord, fly further off!

speaker: PINDARUS
text: Mark Antony is in your tents, my lord.

speaker: PINDARUS
text: Fly therefore, noble Cassius, fly far off.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: This hill is far enough.—Look, look, Titinius,

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Are those my tents where I perceive the fire?

speaker: TITINIUS
text: They are, my lord.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Titinius, if thou lovest me,

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Mount thou my horse and hide thy spurs in him

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Till he have brought thee up to yonder troops

speaker: CASSIUS
text: And here again, that I may rest assured

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Whether yond troops are friend or enemy.

speaker: TITINIUS
text: I will be here again even with a thought.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Go, Pindarus, get higher on that hill.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: My sight was ever thick. Regard Titinius

speaker: CASSIUS
text: And tell me what thou not’st about the field.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: This day I breathèd first. Time is come round,

speaker: CASSIUS
text: And where I did begin, there shall I end;

speaker: CASSIUS
text: My life is run his compass.—Sirrah, what news?

speaker: PINDARUS
text: O my lord!

speaker: CASSIUS
text: What news?

speaker: PINDARUS
text: Titinius is enclosèd round about

speaker: PINDARUS
text: With horsemen that make to him on the spur,

speaker: PINDARUS
text: Yet he spurs on. Now they are almost on him.

speaker: PINDARUS
text: Now Titinius! Now some light. O, he lights too.

speaker: PINDARUS
text: He’s ta’en.

speaker: PINDARUS
text: And hark, they shout for joy.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Come down, behold no more.—

speaker: CASSIUS
text: O, coward that I am to live so long

speaker: CASSIUS
text: To see my best friend ta’en before my face!

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Come hither, sirrah.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: In Parthia did I take thee prisoner,

speaker: CASSIUS
text: And then I swore thee, saving of thy life,

speaker: CASSIUS
text: That whatsoever I did bid thee do

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Thou shouldst attempt it. Come now, keep thine oath.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Now be a freeman, and with this good sword,

speaker: CASSIUS
text: That ran through Caesar’s bowels, search this bosom.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Stand not to answer. Here, take thou the hilts,

speaker: CASSIUS
text: And, when my face is covered, as ’tis now,

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Guide thou the sword.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Caesar, thou art revenged

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Even with the sword that killed thee.

speaker: PINDARUS
text: So I am free, yet would not so have been,

speaker: PINDARUS
text: Durst I have done my will.—O Cassius!—

speaker: PINDARUS
text: Far from this country Pindarus shall run,

speaker: PINDARUS
text: Where never Roman shall take note of him.

speaker: MESSALA
text: It is but change, Titinius, for Octavius

speaker: MESSALA
text: Is overthrown by noble Brutus’ power,

speaker: MESSALA
text: As Cassius’ legions are by Antony.

speaker: TITINIUS
text: These tidings will well comfort Cassius.

speaker: MESSALA
text: Where did you leave him?

speaker: TITINIUS
text: All disconsolate,

speaker: TITINIUS
text: With Pindarus his bondman, on this hill.

speaker: MESSALA
text: Is not that he that lies upon the ground?

speaker: TITINIUS
text: He lies not like the living. O my heart!

speaker: MESSALA
text: Is not that he?

speaker: TITINIUS
text: No, this was he, Messala,

speaker: TITINIUS
text: But Cassius is no more. O setting sun,

speaker: TITINIUS
text: As in thy red rays thou dost sink to night,

speaker: TITINIUS
text: So in his red blood Cassius’ day is set.

speaker: TITINIUS
text: The sun of Rome is set. Our day is gone;

speaker: TITINIUS
text: Clouds, dews, and dangers come. Our deeds are done.

speaker: TITINIUS
text: Mistrust of my success hath done this deed.

speaker: MESSALA
text: Mistrust of good success hath done this deed.

speaker: MESSALA
text: O hateful error, melancholy’s child,

speaker: MESSALA
text: Why dost thou show to the apt thoughts of men

speaker: MESSALA
text: The things that are not? O error, soon conceived,

speaker: MESSALA
text: Thou never com’st unto a happy birth

speaker: MESSALA
text: But kill’st the mother that engendered thee!

speaker: TITINIUS
text: What, Pindarus! Where art thou, Pindarus?

speaker: MESSALA
text: Seek him, Titinius, whilst I go to meet

speaker: MESSALA
text: The noble Brutus, thrusting this report

speaker: MESSALA
text: Into his ears. I may say “thrusting it,”

speaker: MESSALA
text: For piercing steel and darts envenomèd

speaker: MESSALA
text: Shall be as welcome to the ears of Brutus

speaker: MESSALA
text: As tidings of this sight.

speaker: TITINIUS
text: Hie you, Messala,

speaker: TITINIUS
text: And I will seek for Pindarus the while.

speaker: TITINIUS
text: Why didst thou send me forth, brave Cassius?

speaker: TITINIUS
text: Did I not meet thy friends, and did not they

speaker: TITINIUS
text: Put on my brows this wreath of victory

speaker: TITINIUS
text: And bid me give it thee? Didst thou not hear their shouts?

speaker: TITINIUS
text: Alas, thou hast misconstrued everything.

speaker: TITINIUS
text: But hold thee, take this garland on thy brow.

speaker: TITINIUS
text: Thy Brutus bid me give it thee, and I

speaker: TITINIUS
text: Will do his bidding.—Brutus, come apace,

speaker: TITINIUS
text: And see how I regarded Caius Cassius.—

speaker: TITINIUS
text: By your leave, gods, this is a Roman’s part.

speaker: TITINIUS
text: Come, Cassius’ sword, and find Titinius’ heart!

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Where, where, Messala, doth his body lie?

speaker: MESSALA
text: Lo, yonder, and Titinius mourning it.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Titinius’ face is upward.

speaker: CATO
text: He is slain.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: O Julius Caesar, thou art mighty yet;

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Thy spirit walks abroad and turns our swords

speaker: BRUTUS
text: In our own proper entrails.

speaker: CATO
text: Brave Titinius!—

speaker: CATO
text: Look whe’er he have not crowned dead Cassius.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Are yet two Romans living such as these?—

speaker: BRUTUS
text: The last of all the Romans, fare thee well.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: It is impossible that ever Rome

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Should breed thy fellow.—Friends, I owe more tears

speaker: BRUTUS
text: To this dead man than you shall see me pay.—

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I shall find time, Cassius; I shall find time.—

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Come, therefore, and to Thasos send his body.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: His funerals shall not be in our camp,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Lest it discomfort us.—Lucilius, come.—

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And come, young Cato. Let us to the field.—

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Labeo and Flavius, set our battles on.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: ’Tis three o’clock, and, Romans, yet ere night

speaker: BRUTUS
text: We shall try fortune in a second fight.
